description:
MCPRemoteProxy is the Schema for the mcpremoteproxies API
It enables proxying remote MCP servers with authentication, authorization, audit logging, and tool filtering

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: MCPRemoteProxySpec defines the desired state of MCPRemoteProxy

audit.description: Audit defines audit logging configuration for the proxy

enabled.description:
Enabled controls whether audit logging is enabled
When true, enables audit logging with default configuration

authServerRef.description:
AuthServerRef optionally references a resource that configures an embedded
OAuth 2.0/OIDC authorization server to authenticate MCP clients.
Currently the only supported kind is MCPExternalAuthConfig (type: embeddedAuthServer).

kind.description: Kind identifies the type of the referenced resource.

name.description: Name is the name of the referenced resource in the same namespace.

authzConfig.description: AuthzConfig defines authorization policy configuration for the proxy

configMap.description:
ConfigMap references a ConfigMap containing authorization configuration
Only used when Type is "configMap"

key.description: Key is the key in the ConfigMap that contains the authorization configuration

name.description: Name is the name of the ConfigMap

inline.description:
Inline contains direct authorization configuration
Only used when Type is "inline"

entitiesJson.description: EntitiesJSON is a JSON string representing Cedar entities

policies.description: Policies is a list of Cedar policy strings

type.description: Type is the type of authorization configuration

x-kubernetes-validations.message: configMap must be set when type is 'configMap', and must not be set otherwise
inline must be set when type is 'inline', and must not be set otherwise

endpointPrefix.description:
EndpointPrefix is the path prefix to prepend to SSE endpoint URLs.
This is used to handle path-based ingress routing scenarios where the ingress
strips a path prefix before forwarding to the backend.

externalAuthConfigRef.description:
ExternalAuthConfigRef references a MCPExternalAuthConfig resource for token exchange.
When specified, the proxy will exchange validated incoming tokens for remote service tokens.
The referenced MCPExternalAuthConfig must exist in the same namespace as this MCPRemoteProxy.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPExternalAuthConfig resource

groupRef.description:
GroupRef is the name of the MCPGroup this proxy belongs to
Must reference an existing MCPGroup in the same namespace

headerForward.description:
HeaderForward configures headers to inject into requests to the remote MCP server.
Use this to add custom headers like X-Tenant-ID or correlation IDs.

addHeadersFromSecret.description: AddHeadersFromSecret references Kubernetes Secrets for sensitive header values.

items.description: HeaderFromSecret defines a header whose value comes from a Kubernetes Secret.

headerName.description: HeaderName is the HTTP header name (e.g., "X-API-Key")

valueSecretRef.description: ValueSecretRef references the Secret and key containing the header value

key.description: Key is the key within the secret

name.description: Name is the name of the secret

addPlaintextHeaders.description:
AddPlaintextHeaders is a map of header names to literal values to inject into requests.
WARNING: Values are stored in plaintext and visible via kubectl commands.
Use addHeadersFromSecret for sensitive data like API keys or tokens.

oidcConfig.description:
OIDCConfig defines OIDC authentication configuration for the proxy.
Deprecated: Use OIDCConfigRef to reference a shared MCPOIDCConfig resource instead.
This field will be removed in v1beta1. OIDCConfig and OIDCConfigRef are mutually exclusive.

configMap.description:
ConfigMap references a ConfigMap containing OIDC configuration
Only used when Type is "configmap"

caBundleRef.description:
CABundleRef references a ConfigMap containing the CA certificate bundle.
When specified, ToolHive auto-mounts the ConfigMap and auto-computes ThvCABundlePath.
If the ConfigMap data contains an explicit thvCABundlePath key, it takes precedence.

configMapRef.description:
ConfigMapRef references a ConfigMap containing the CA certificate bundle.
If Key is not specified, it defaults to "ca.crt".

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined

key.description: Key is the key in the ConfigMap that contains the OIDC configuration

name.description: Name is the name of the ConfigMap

inline.description:
Inline contains direct OIDC configuration
Only used when Type is "inline"

audience.description: Audience is the expected audience for the token

caBundleRef.description:
CABundleRef references a ConfigMap containing the CA certificate bundle.
When specified, ToolHive auto-mounts the ConfigMap and auto-computes the CA bundle path.

configMapRef.description:
ConfigMapRef references a ConfigMap containing the CA certificate bundle.
If Key is not specified, it defaults to "ca.crt".

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined

clientId.description: ClientID is the OIDC client ID

clientSecretRef.description: ClientSecretRef is a reference to a Kubernetes Secret containing the client secret

key.description: Key is the key within the secret

name.description: Name is the name of the secret

insecureAllowHTTP.description:
InsecureAllowHTTP allows HTTP (non-HTTPS) OIDC issuers for development/testing
WARNING: This is insecure and should NEVER be used in production
Only enable for local development, testing, or trusted internal networks

introspectionUrl.description: IntrospectionURL is the URL for token introspection endpoint

issuer.description: Issuer is the OIDC issuer URL

jwksAllowPrivateIP.description:
JWKSAllowPrivateIP allows JWKS/OIDC endpoints on private IP addresses
Use with caution - only enable for trusted internal IDPs

jwksAuthTokenPath.description:
JWKSAuthTokenPath is the path to file containing bearer token for JWKS/OIDC requests
The file must be mounted into the pod (e.g., via Secret volume)

jwksUrl.description: JWKSURL is the URL to fetch the JWKS from

protectedResourceAllowPrivateIP.description:
ProtectedResourceAllowPrivateIP allows protected resource endpoint on private IP addresses
Use with caution - only enable for trusted internal IDPs or testing

scopes.description:
Scopes is the list of OAuth scopes to advertise in the well-known endpoint (RFC 9728)
If empty, defaults to ["openid"]

kubernetes.description:
Kubernetes configures OIDC for Kubernetes service account token validation
Only used when Type is "kubernetes"

audience.description: Audience is the expected audience for the token

introspectionUrl.description:
IntrospectionURL is the URL for token introspection endpoint
If empty, OIDC discovery will be used to automatically determine the introspection URL

issuer.description: Issuer is the OIDC issuer URL

jwksUrl.description:
JWKSURL is the URL to fetch the JWKS from
If empty, OIDC discovery will be used to automatically determine the JWKS URL

namespace.description:
Namespace is the namespace of the service account
If empty, uses the MCPServer's namespace

serviceAccount.description:
ServiceAccount is the name of the service account to validate tokens for
If empty, uses the pod's service account

useClusterAuth.description:
UseClusterAuth enables using the Kubernetes cluster's CA bundle and service account token
When true, uses /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt for TLS verification
and /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token for bearer token authentication
Defaults to true if not specified

resourceUrl.description:
ResourceURL is the explicit resource URL for OAuth discovery endpoint (RFC 9728)
If not specified, defaults to the in-cluster Kubernetes service URL

type.description: Type is the type of OIDC configuration

x-kubernetes-validations.message: configMap must be set when type is 'configMap', and must not be set otherwise
inline must be set when type is 'inline', and must not be set otherwise
kubernetes must not be set when type is not 'kubernetes'

oidcConfigRef.description:
OIDCConfigRef references a shared MCPOIDCConfig resource for OIDC authentication.
The referenced MCPOIDCConfig must exist in the same namespace as this MCPRemoteProxy.
Per-server overrides (audience, scopes) are specified here; shared provider config
lives in the MCPOIDCConfig resource.

audience.description:
Audience is the expected audience for token validation.
This MUST be unique per server to prevent token replay attacks.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPOIDCConfig resource

scopes.description:
Scopes is the list of OAuth scopes to advertise in the well-known endpoint (RFC 9728).
If empty, defaults to ["openid"].

proxyPort.description: ProxyPort is the port to expose the MCP proxy on

remoteUrl.description: RemoteURL is the URL of the remote MCP server to proxy

resourceOverrides.description: ResourceOverrides allows overriding annotations and labels for resources created by the operator

proxyDeployment.description: ProxyDeployment defines overrides for the Proxy Deployment resource (toolhive proxy)

annotations.description: Annotations to add or override on the resource

env.description:
Env are environment variables to set in the proxy container (thv run process)
These affect the toolhive proxy itself, not the MCP server it manages
Use TOOLHIVE_DEBUG=true to enable debug logging in the proxy

items.description: EnvVar represents an environment variable in a container

imagePullSecrets.description:
ImagePullSecrets allows specifying image pull secrets for the proxy runner
These are applied to both the Deployment and the ServiceAccount

items.description:
LocalObjectReference contains enough information to let you locate the
referenced object inside the same namespace.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

labels.description: Labels to add or override on the resource

podTemplateMetadataOverrides.description: ResourceMetadataOverrides defines metadata overrides for a resource

annotations.description: Annotations to add or override on the resource

labels.description: Labels to add or override on the resource

proxyService.description: ProxyService defines overrides for the Proxy Service resource (points to the proxy deployment)

annotations.description: Annotations to add or override on the resource

labels.description: Labels to add or override on the resource

resources.description: Resources defines the resource requirements for the proxy container

limits.description: Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed

cpu.description: CPU is the CPU limit in cores (e.g., "500m" for 0.5 cores)

memory.description: Memory is the memory limit in bytes (e.g., "64Mi" for 64 megabytes)

requests.description: Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required

cpu.description: CPU is the CPU limit in cores (e.g., "500m" for 0.5 cores)

memory.description: Memory is the memory limit in bytes (e.g., "64Mi" for 64 megabytes)

serviceAccount.description:
ServiceAccount is the name of an already existing service account to use by the proxy.
If not specified, a ServiceAccount will be created automatically and used by the proxy.

sessionAffinity.description:
SessionAffinity controls whether the Service routes repeated client connections to the same pod.
MCP protocols (SSE, streamable-http) are stateful, so ClientIP is the default.
Set to "None" for stateless servers or when using an external load balancer with its own affinity.

telemetry.description:
Telemetry defines inline observability configuration for the proxy.
Deprecated: Use TelemetryConfigRef to reference a shared MCPTelemetryConfig resource instead.
This field will be removed in a future release. Setting both telemetry and telemetryConfigRef
is rejected by CEL validation.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether OpenTelemetry is enabled

endpoint.description: Endpoint is the OTLP endpoint URL for tracing and metrics

headers.description:
Headers contains authentication headers for the OTLP endpoint
Specified as key=value pairs

insecure.description: Insecure indicates whether to use HTTP instead of HTTPS for the OTLP endpoint

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether OTLP metrics are sent

serviceName.description:
ServiceName is the service name for telemetry
If not specified, defaults to the MCPServer name

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether OTLP tracing is sent

samplingRate.description: SamplingRate is the trace sampling rate (0.0-1.0)

useLegacyAttributes.description:
UseLegacyAttributes controls whether legacy attribute names are emitted alongside
the new MCP OTEL semantic convention names. Defaults to true for backward compatibility.
This will change to false in a future release and eventually be removed.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether Prometheus metrics endpoint is exposed

telemetryConfigRef.description:
TelemetryConfigRef references an MCPTelemetryConfig resource for shared telemetry configuration.
The referenced MCPTelemetryConfig must exist in the same namespace as this MCPRemoteProxy.
Cross-namespace references are not supported for security and isolation reasons.
Mutually exclusive with the deprecated inline Telemetry field.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPTelemetryConfig resource

serviceName.description:
ServiceName overrides the telemetry service name for this specific server.
This MUST be unique per server for proper observability (e.g., distinguishing
traces and metrics from different servers sharing the same collector).
If empty, defaults to the server name with "thv-" prefix at runtime.

toolConfigRef.description:
ToolConfigRef references a MCPToolConfig resource for tool filtering and renaming.
The referenced MCPToolConfig must exist in the same namespace as this MCPRemoteProxy.
Cross-namespace references are not supported for security and isolation reasons.
If specified, this allows filtering and overriding tools from the remote MCP server.

name.description: Name is the name of the MCPToolConfig resource in the same namespace

transport.description: Transport is the transport method for the remote proxy (sse or streamable-http)

trustProxyHeaders.description:
TrustProxyHeaders indicates whether to trust X-Forwarded-* headers from reverse proxies
When enabled, the proxy will use X-Forwarded-Proto, X-Forwarded-Host, X-Forwarded-Port,
and X-Forwarded-Prefix headers to construct endpoint URLs

x-kubernetes-validations.message: oidcConfig and oidcConfigRef are mutually exclusive; use oidcConfigRef to reference a shared MCPOIDCConfig
telemetry and telemetryConfigRef are mutually exclusive; migrate to telemetryConfigRef

status.description: MCPRemoteProxyStatus defines the observed state of MCPRemoteProxy

authServerConfigHash.description:
AuthServerConfigHash is the hash of the referenced authServerRef spec,
used to detect configuration changes and trigger reconciliation.

conditions.description: Conditions represent the latest available observations of the MCPRemoteProxy's state

items.description: Condition contains details for one aspect of the current state of this API Resource.

lastTransitionTime.description:
lastTransitionTime is the last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.
This should be when the underlying condition changed.  If that is not known, then using the time when the API field changed is acceptable.

message.description:
message is a human readable message indicating details about the transition.
This may be an empty string.

observedGeneration.description:
observedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon.
For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions[x].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date
with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason.description:
reason contains a programmatic identifier indicating the reason for the condition's last transition.
Producers of specific condition types may define expected values and meanings for this field,
and whether the values are considered a guaranteed API.
The value should be a CamelCase string.
This field may not be empty.

status.description: status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

type.description: type of condition in CamelCase or in foo.example.com/CamelCase.

externalAuthConfigHash.description: ExternalAuthConfigHash is the hash of the referenced MCPExternalAuthConfig spec

externalUrl.description: ExternalURL is the external URL where the proxy can be accessed (if exposed externally)

message.description: Message provides additional information about the current phase

observedGeneration.description: ObservedGeneration reflects the generation of the most recently observed MCPRemoteProxy

oidcConfigHash.description: OIDCConfigHash is the hash of the referenced MCPOIDCConfig spec for change detection

phase.description: Phase is the current phase of the MCPRemoteProxy

telemetryConfigHash.description: TelemetryConfigHash stores the hash of the referenced MCPTelemetryConfig for change detection

toolConfigHash.description: ToolConfigHash stores the hash of the referenced ToolConfig for change detection

url.description: URL is the internal cluster URL where the proxy can be accessed
